message:
fix(raptorq): order-independent multiset hash, ESI cursor docs, schema helpers

Three related improvements to the RaptorQ subsystem:

1. proof.rs — The received-ESI multiset hash previously sorted a Vec of
   (esi, is_source) pairs and hashed the sorted sequence.  This was
   O(n log n) and allocation-heavy for large symbol counts.  Replaced with
   an incremental `ReceivedEsiMultisetHashState` that combines per-element
   digests via commutative operations (wrapping add, rotate-xor), producing
   a stable fingerprint regardless of symbol arrival order.  Added a
   dedicated test (`received_summary_hash_is_order_independent_for_same_multiset`)
   proving order-independence.

2. systematic.rs — Corrected doc-comments for the repair emission cursor:
   repair ESIs start at K (not K'), matching RFC 6330 wire encoding.  The
   internal ISI translation `X = ESI + (K' - K)` is now documented at the
   struct level so callers understand the mapping without reading the impl.

3. test_log_schema.rs — Extracted duplicate artifact_path validation logic
   into a reusable `validate_optional_non_empty_string_field` helper,
   removing the `clippy::too_many_lines` allow on `validate_unit_log_json`.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
